inline_comments.message: NB: you can provide a direct value for this 'org.postgresql.Driver' since it doesn't read from any properies file!
NB: can use single qoutes instead double for groovy strings.

Separation of Concerns

DataSourceProperties → Handles database connection settings
JpaProperties → Handles Hibernate-related settings

I disagree. The concern is to populate HibernateConfiguration from application.yml.

This way is a lot of extra code for a simple problem: we would be going from a single class containing a single bean to 4 different classes.
(The fourth class is needed because "new_generator_mappings" is not directly under "spring.jpa.properties.hibernate")
Hi Sourabh, can you explain the intent of this comment/codeblock
you mean you can group proreties together this way and reduce the number of annotations etc?

NB: the method does more now then just getting a factory
getSessionFactoryWithHibernateConfiguration()
